commit.message:
fix: set FUZZ_ICC_DIR in native coverage build

The native coverage build was missing FUZZ_ICC_DIR, causing all ICC
embedding, mutation, and color space diversity functions to be skipped
(0% coverage). This left 25 of 68 functions in xnuimagefuzzer.m
uncovered (63.24% → expected ~80%+ after fix).

Set FUZZ_ICC_DIR=/System/Library/ColorSync/Profiles to exercise:
- embedICCProfile() / embedICCProfileData()
- mutateICCProfile() (6 corruption strategies)
- createNamedColorSpace() (7 named color spaces)
- encodeImageWithICCProfile()
- loadICCProfilePaths()
- All 4 ICC variant paths (clean, stripped, mismatched, mutated)

Also softened the file count assertion since ICC variants may not
produce files on all platforms.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix browser apps showing wrong category in statistics

Browser apps (Safari, Chrome, etc.) were assigned the category of their
first activity record, ignoring all subsequent activities. If the first
page visited was YouTube (Distraction), the entire browser showed as
Distraction in stats even if 90% of browsing was productive.

Fix: use the dominant category (most total duration) per app in the
app usage aggregation. This correctly reflects actual browsing patterns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
consensus: Dynamic block production quorum (1/3 active masternodes, floor 3)

Previously hardcoded to require exactly 3 peers before producing a block.
Now requires max(active_masternodes/3, 3) connected peers, scaling with
network size. On a 7-node testnet this is still 3; on a 100-node mainnet
it would require 34 peers, preventing isolated minorities from forking.

commit.message:
feat: implement buffer ring management for double/triple buffering

Implement Step 8 from PLAN.md - Buffer Double/Triple Buffering Foundation.

Created internal/buffer package with Ring type that manages a ring of
buffer slots cycling through states: available → rendering → displaying
→ released → available. This enables smooth frame presentation without
blocking the render thread.

Features:
- State machine with proper lifecycle management
- Context-aware acquisition with timeout/cancellation support
- Non-blocking release signaling safe for event handlers
- Thread-safe with mutex protection
- UserData field for platform-specific handles
- Support for 2+ buffer slots (double/triple/quad buffering)
- Reset() for VT switch recovery scenarios

Implementation details:
- internal/buffer/ring.go: 267 LOC, 14 public functions/methods
- internal/buffer/ring_test.go: 427 LOC, 18 tests + 2 benchmarks
- Zero functions with cyclomatic complexity > 10 (max CC = 7)
- 100% documentation coverage (all functions, types, methods)
- All tests passing with zero regressions

Designed for both Wayland (wl_buffer.release events) and X11
(Present idle/complete notifications).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
xnuimagefuzzer: add 8 TIFF IFD structure-aware mutations

Replace the minimal 3-line TIFF corruption stub with 8 structure-aware
mutation strategies modeled after the existing PNG mutations:

  0. IFD entry count corruption (0, 0xFFFF, 3000, etc.)
  1. IFD tag type field corruption (invalid TIFF data types)
  2. StripOffsets/StripByteCounts out-of-bounds values
  3. IFD entry reordering (violates ascending tag requirement)
  4. Circular next-IFD pointer (infinite loop trigger)
  5. ICC Profile tag (34675) count corruption (OOB read)
  6. BigTIFF magic injection (0x002B in standard TIFF)
  7. Image dimension corruption (width/height tags 256/257)

All mutations are byte-order aware (II/MM) and respect the TIFF
header structure. These target libtiff, ImageIO, CoreGraphics,
and ColorSync parsing paths.

Synced from xnuimagetools commit bc99abb.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix group item positions on copy/paste and duplicate (#131)

* Initial plan

* Fix group item positions on copy/paste and duplicate

When deserializing or duplicating QGraphicsItemGroup items (e.g. arrows),
child positions were being double-transformed by addToGroup().  Children
are serialized with group-local coordinates, but addToGroup() treated
them as scene coordinates and remapped them, causing items to shift or
disappear.

Fix: add children to the group BEFORE setting the group's pos/transform
so that addToGroup() sees the group at the origin with identity
transform, making the scene-to-group mapping an identity operation.

Co-authored-by: djeada <37275728+djeada@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Paste items at current mouse position instead of fixed offset/viewport center (#133)

* Initial plan

* Make paste operation place items at current mouse position

Instead of using a fixed (20,20) offset for internal canvas items or
the viewport center for images/text, pasteItems() now queries
QCursor::pos() at paste time. If the cursor is inside the viewport,
items are centered on that scene position; otherwise the viewport
centre is used as a fallback.

For multi-item canvas pastes the collective bounding-rect centre is
moved to the mouse position, preserving the relative layout of all
pasted items.

* Use sceneBoundingRect() for more accurate bounds computation during paste

commit.message:
GCU generates suboptimal plan for CreateOnly paths (#4335)

* GCU generates suboptimal plan for CreateOnly paths

When GCU hits a CreateOnly entry that has changed, it generates a suboptimal
plan.  One example is a simple change of:
```
[{"op": "replace", "path": "/MIRROR_SESSION/EVERFLOW_TUNNEL/dst_ip", "value": "200.1.1.203"}]
```

Should generate an optimal plan of:
```
[
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/MIRROR_SESSION"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/MIRROR_SESSION", "value": {"EVERFLOW_TUNNEL": {"dscp": "8", "dst_ip": "200.1.1.203", "src_ip": "100.1.1.1", "ttl": "255", "type": "ERSPAN"}}}]
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION", "value": "EVERFLOW_TUNNEL"}]
]
```

But instead generates this plan (which removes all ACLs):
```
[
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1", "value": {"PRIORITY": "1000"}}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION", "value": "EVERFLOW_TUNNEL"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE", "value": {"EVERFLOW|RULE_1": {"PRIORITY": "1000", "IP_TYPE": "IP", "MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION": "EVERFLOW_TUNNEL"}}}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1", "value": {"PRIORITY": "10"}}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/DST_IP", "value": "192.168.1.1/32"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/IP_TYPE", "value": "IP"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/L4_DST_PORT", "value": "22"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1", "value": {"PRIORITY": "1000"}}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION", "value": "EVERFLOW_TUNNEL"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1", "value": {"PRIORITY": "10"}}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/DST_IP", "value": "192.168.1.1/32"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/IP_TYPE", "value": "IP"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/PACKET_ACTION", "value": "DROP"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/IP_TYPE", "value": "IP"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1", "value": {"PRIORITY": "1000"}}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/IP_TYPE", "value": "IP"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1", "value": {"PRIORITY": "10"}}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/DST_IP", "value": "192.168.1.1/32"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/IP_TYPE", "value": "IP"}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1", "value": {"PRIORITY": "1000"}}],
[{"op": "remove", "path": "/MIRROR_SESSION"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/MIRROR_SESSION", "value": {"EVERFLOW_TUNNEL": {"dscp": "8", "dst_ip": "200.1.1.203", "src_ip": "100.1.1.1", "ttl": "255", "type": "ERSPAN"}}}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/L4_DST_PORT", "value": "22"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/DATAACL|RULE_1/PACKET_ACTION", "value": "DROP"}],
[{"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/IP_TYPE", "value": "IP"}, {"op": "add", "path": "/ACL_RULE/EVERFLOW|RULE_1/MIRROR_INGRESS_ACTION", "value": "EVERFLOW_TUNNEL"}]
]
```

Modified`RemoveCreateOnlyDependencyMoveGenerator`:
 * it would previously short-circuit early due to only processing one child
   leaf in the same table.
 * it would previously attempt to iterate across all members of the table even
   though there was a complete path list.
 * it was missing logic to remove the create only path itself (and was relying
   on extenders to do that which was inefficient and wouldn't generate the
   right plan)
 * when removing dependents it wasn't recursing to ensure it would remove
   dependents of dependents

Since this generator is now full and doesn't rely on any extenders, it has
been moved to a non-extendable generator.

These changes caused some existing (suboptimal) plans that got generated to
change so those test cases have also been updated.

Added test case to validate this behavior and ensure it does not regress.

Signed-off-by: Brad House <bhouse@nexthop.ai>

* Update generic_config_updater/patch_sorter.py

Prevent possible infinite loop scenario Copilot identified,
however it shouldn't be possible given self.__get_path_count()
can't return 1 in that scenario to allow the loop to continue.
But hardening isn't a bad practice.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Brad House <bhouse@nexthop.ai>

* Update generic_config_updater/patch_sorter.py

Fix spelling / gramatical error caught by Copilot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Brad House <bhouse@nexthop.ai>

* Code Review Comments

1. Add recursion depth protection in removing dependents because of
   a concern of recursive dependencies, which isn't actually possible
   with yang.  None-the-less, implemented.
2. Remove a duplicate move that gets generated as when using it with a
   Depth-first sorter its not necessary though could be on other sorters.
3. The old code depended on 3 levels of depth for the create only
   leafs.  Reworked the logic to not be dependent on depth.
4. __get_path_count() can no longer return a KeyError even though the
   caller paths would make that impossible, but future use cases
   may need it to not throw an exception when the path is not found.

Signed-off-by: Brad House <bhouse@nexthop.ai>

Shard miri-harder jobs to run in parallel (#132)

## Summary

Shards the `miri-harder` CI jobs so they run in parallel across multiple
runners instead of sequentially on a single runner.

## Changes

### Just recipe (`justfiles/just_testing.just`)
- Added optional `SHARD` parameter to `miri-harder` (e.g., `just
package=foo miri-harder 1/4`).
- Splits the 64-seed range (`-Zmiri-many-seeds=..64`) across shards:
  - `1/4` → seeds `0..16`, `2/4` → seeds `16..32`, etc.
- Without a shard parameter, all 64 seeds run as before (backward
compatible).
- Works with any shard count (e.g., `1/3` also works).
- Validates shard input with clear error messages for malformed values.

### GitHub workflow (`.github/workflows/validation.yml`)
- `miri-harder-events-once` uses a 4-shard matrix (timeout 60 min per
shard).
- `miri-harder-infinity-pool` uses an 8-shard matrix (timeout 60 min per
shard).
- `fail-fast: false` ensures all shards run independently.

### Documentation (`.github/workflows/AGENTS.md`)
- Updated to reflect the new sharded design.

- Minimal async overhead (< 2μs)
- Linear sequential scaling
- Sub-linear parallel scaling

- 🚧 14 benchmarks need API corrections (Retry, Cache, Conditional, Fallback)

- Add memory profiling
- CI/CD integration with regression detection

commit.message:
Rewrite: the prompt IS the component boundary

Key insight: when you prompt AI, the granularity of your prompt
naturally maps to a component. Design components so that prompting
their implementation is all it takes. If it doesn't work, rewrite
it — the rest of the system never knows.
